article_items.title: Fall 2025 Amazon Research Awards recipients announced
article_items.description: Awardees represent more than 49 universities in 11 countries. Recipients have access to Amazon public datasets, along with AWS AI/ML services and tools.
article_items.excerpt: Awardees represent more than 49 universities in 11 countries. Recipients have access to Amazon public datasets, along with AWS AI/ML services and tools.
article_items.summary: The tables below list, in alphabetical order by last name, fall 2025 cycle call-for-proposal recipients, sorted by research CortexCTI: A Unified Threat Intelligence Engine for Knowledge-Driven Cloud Threat Detection and Response New Benchmark and Defense on Prompt Injection in Agentic AI Systems Securing Agentic AI: From Local Detection to Global Assurance Exploit-driven AI Agents for vulnerability detection verification Securing Agentic AI: From Local Detection to Global Assurance SOLAR: Symbolic Learning for. This announcement includes awards funded under 6 calls for proposals during the fall 2025 cycle: AI for Information Security , Agentic AI , Automated Reasoning , AWS Cryptography , Cybersecurity and Anti-Abuse Technologies , and Sustainability Proposals were reviewed for the quality of their scientific content and their potential to impact both the research community and society. Since 2020, our team has funded security. Applicants are encouraged to visit our call for proposals page for more information or send an email to be notified of future open calls. Fall 2025 Amazon Research Awards recipients announced Awardees represent more than 49 universities in 11 countries.

Additionally, Amazon encourages the publication of research results, presentations of research at Amazon offices worldwide, and the release of related code under open-source licenses.
Fall 2025 Amazon Research Awards recipients announced Awardees represent more than 49 universities in 11 countries.
This cycle, ARA received many excellent research proposals from across the world and today is publicly announcing 68 award recipients who represent 49 universities in 11 countries.
Publications Research from our scientists and collaborators.

This announcement includes awards funded under 6 calls for proposals during the fall 2025 cycle: AI for Information Security , Agentic AI , Automated Reasoning , AWS Cryptography , Cybersecurity and Anti-Abuse.
Since 2020, our team has funded security research that solves some of the biggest challenges for the industry.

Security, privacy, and abuse prevention Verifying and optimizing post-quantum cryptography at Amazon Hanno Becker , Rod Chapman , Dusan Kostic April 7, 2026 How automated reasoning reconciles the demands of security.
Recipients also are assigned an Amazon research contact who offers consultation and advice, along with opportunities to participate in Amazon events and training sessions.
Recipients have access to more than 700 Amazon public datasets and can utilize AWS AI/ML services and tools through their AWS Promotional Credits.
Automated reasoning Amazon.com | Conditions of Use | Privacy | 1996-2026 Amazon.com, Inc.
Amazon Research Awards (ARA) provides unrestricted funds and AWS Promotional Credits to academic researchers investigating various research topics in multiple disciplines.

article_items.title: ITBench-AA: Frontier Models Score Below 50% on the First Benchmark for Agentic Enterprise IT Tasks — by Artificial Analysis and IBM
article_items.description: A Blog post by IBM Research on Hugging Face
article_items.excerpt: A Blog post by IBM Research on Hugging Face
article_items.summary: The harness (Stirrup) is held constant across all evaluated models, allowing an apples-to-apples comparison between models. A Blog post by IBM Research on Hugging Face ITBench-AA: Frontier Models Score Below 50% on the First Benchmark for Agentic Enterprise IT Tasks - by Artificial Analysis and IBM ITBench-AA is built in partnership with @IBM based on their ITBench benchmark. In one public SRE task, the agent sees user-facing failures in the frontend path. More turns do not mean better answers. ITBench-AA is built in partnership with @IBM based on their ITBench benchmark. Claude Opus 4.7 (Adaptive Reasoning, Max Effort) leads the leaderboard at 47% but is the most expensive at $5.38 per task.
article_items.what_happened: The harness (Stirrup) is held constant across all evaluated models, allowing an apples-to-apples comparison between models. A Blog post by IBM Research on Hugging Face ITBench-AA: Frontier Models Score Below 50% on the First Benchmark for Agentic Enterprise IT Tasks - by Artificial Analysis and IBM ITBench-AA is built in partnership with @IBM based on their ITBench benchmark.
article_items.why_matters: In one public SRE task, the agent sees user-facing failures in the frontend path. More turns do not mean better answers.
article_items.what_to_watch: ITBench-AA is built in partnership with @IBM based on their ITBench benchmark. Claude Opus 4.7 (Adaptive Reasoning, Max Effort) leads the leaderboard at 47% but is the most expensive at $5.38 per task.
key_points: A Blog post by IBM Research on Hugging Face ITBench-AA: Frontier Models Score Below 50% on the First Benchmark for Agentic Enterprise IT Tasks - by Artificial Analysis and IBM ITBench-AA is built in partnership with.
The harness (Stirrup) is held constant across all evaluated models, allowing an apples-to-apples comparison between models.
Claude Opus 4.7 (Adaptive Reasoning, Max Effort) leads the leaderboard at 47% but is the most expensive at $5.38 per task.
The headline score is the average across 59 tasks × 3 repeats.
This is why some models with long trajectories underperform terser ones: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview averages 83 turns and scores 30%, while Gemma 4 31B (Reasoning) averages 58 turns and scores 37%.
DeepSeek V4 Pro (Reasoning, Max Effort) follows at 38%, with Gemma 4 31B (Reasoning) at 37%, ahead of Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview at 30%.
Gemma 4 31B (Reasoning) scores 37% at $0.14 per task, outperforming Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview ($2.23 per task, 30%) on both score and cost.
GLM-5.1 (Reasoning) leads open weights models at 40%, effectively tied with Gemini 3.5 Flash (high).
For more information see: ITBench paper on arXiv: ITBench-AA leaderboard: ITBench-AA HuggingFace repo: The Open Agent Leaderboard Inside VAKRA: Reasoning, Tool Use, and Failure Modes of Agents
All frontier models score below 50%, making ITBench-AA SRE one of the least saturated agentic benchmarks in our suite.
The model must identify the minimal set of independent root-cause Kubernetes entities responsible for the incident.
GLM-5.1 (Reasoning) scores 40% at $1.23 per task, matching Gemini 3.5 Flash (high) ($1.70) on score at lower cost.

article_items.title: NVIDIA Releases Polar, a Token-Faithful Rollout Framework for GRPO Training Across Codex, Claude Code, and Qwen Code
article_items.description: NVIDIA's Polar proxies LLM API calls to reconstruct token-faithful RL trajectories for training agents on unmodified coding harnesses.
article_items.excerpt: NVIDIA's Polar proxies LLM API calls to reconstruct token-faithful RL trajectories for training agents on unmodified coding harnesses.
article_items.summary: Connect with us Sakana AI Proposes DiffusionBlocks: a Block-wise Training Framework That Converts Residual Networks into Independently Trainable Denoising Modules Meet EAGLE 3.1: The Speculative Decoding Algorithm That Fixes Attention Drift in LLM Inference MEMO: A Modular Framework for Training a Dedicated Memory Model on New Knowledge Without Modifying LLM Parameters Design a High-Precision Retrieve-and-Rerank Pipeline with ZeroEntropy Zerank-2 Reranker Stability AI Releases Stable Audio 3: A Family of Fast. NVIDIA’s research team introduced Polar , a rollout framework that lets researchers run reinforcement learning over any agent harness without modifying that harness. The research team benchmarks both strategies on the same model, hardware, and topology over three training steps. NVIDIA Releases Polar, a Token-Faithful Rollout Framework for GRPO Training Across Codex, Claude Code, and Qwen Code Reinforcement learning for language agents is growing more complex.

Strengths and Limitations No harness code changes required - the proxy intercepts at the model API boundary Provider-agnostic: supports Anthropic, OpenAI Chat, OpenAI Responses, and Google API formats natively.
NVIDIA Releases Polar, a Token-Faithful Rollout Framework for GRPO Training Across Codex, Claude Code, and Qwen Code Reinforcement learning for language agents is growing more complex.
Meet EAGLE 3.1: The Speculative Decoding Algorithm That Fixes Attention Drift in LLM Inference MEMO: A Modular Framework for Training a Dedicated Memory Model on New Knowledge Without...
NVIDIA’s research team introduced Polar , a rollout framework that lets researchers run reinforcement learning over any agent harness without modifying that harness.
The research team benchmarks both strategies on the same model, hardware, and topology over three training steps.
The research team demonstrates this using Qwen3.5-122B-A10B on an 8×H100 server (TP=8, max_model_len=32,768) with the pi harness against 1,638 instances from seven SWE-Gym repositories.
Offline SFT Data Generation Polar can also serve as a distributed offline data generation service with no changes to the runtime.
SWE-Bench Verified Results Training uses standard GRPO on the Qwen3.5-4B base model.
Under the native Qwen Code harness, where the base model is already well-aligned, Polar still delivers a 0.6 point gain.
NVIDIA's Polar proxies LLM API calls to reconstruct token-faithful RL trajectories for training agents on unmodified coding harnesses.

article_items.title: Introducing Music v2, our groundbreaking new music model
article_items.description: Better vocals, instrumentation, and arrangement across every genre, improved multilingual support plus capabilities that weren't possible before.
article_items.excerpt: Better vocals, instrumentation, and arrangement across every genre, improved multilingual support plus capabilities that weren't possible before.
article_items.summary: Music v2 powers three ElevenLabs platforms, each built for a different use case: ElevenCreative - downloadable music for ads, branded content, and video Alongside this release, we've cut both Music v1 and Music v2 pricing by up to 50% for ElevenAPI and up to 40% for ElevenCreative self-serve customers. Introducing Eleven Multilingual v1: Our New Speech Synthesis Model This Voice Doesn't Exist - Generative Voice AI The first AI that can laugh Whether you're creating music, building a product, or making content at scale, there's a purpose-built platform for you. Reach out to our Sales team to enquire about early access. The model is trained only on licensed data and cleared for commercial use, so every track you generate is yours to use - no sync fees, no clearance delays, no restrictions on how you deploy it.
article_items.what_happened: Music v2 powers three ElevenLabs platforms, each built for a different use case: ElevenCreative - downloadable music for ads, branded content, and video Alongside this release, we've cut both Music v1 and Music v2 pricing by up to 50% for ElevenAPI and up to 40% for ElevenCreative self-serve customers. Introducing Eleven Multilingual v1: Our New Speech Synthesis Model This Voice Doesn't Exist - Generative Voice AI The first AI that can laugh
article_items.why_matters: Whether you're creating music, building a product, or making content at scale, there's a purpose-built platform for you. Reach out to our Sales team to enquire about early access.
article_items.what_to_watch: The model is trained only on licensed data and cleared for commercial use, so every track you generate is yours to use - no sync fees, no clearance delays, no restrictions on how you deploy it.
key_points: Music v2 powers three ElevenLabs platforms, each built for a different use case: ElevenCreative - downloadable music for ads, branded content, and video Alongside this release, we've cut both Music v1 and Music v2.
Introducing Eleven Multilingual v1: Our New Speech Synthesis Model This Voice Doesn't Exist - Generative Voice AI The first AI that can laugh
The model is trained only on licensed data and cleared for commercial use, so every track you generate is yours to use - no sync fees, no clearance delays, no restrictions on how you deploy it.

Music v2 introduces a new set of capabilities and improvements when compared to our previous model, from new ways to control and shape a track, to handling vocal and compositional complexity at a level that wasn't.
This release also builds on growing alignment across the music industry around licensed and rights-respecting AI music development, including recent licensing collaborations with Believe.
Introducing Music v2 Music v2 delivers better vocals, instrumentation, and arrangement across every genre, with improved multilingual support and a set of new capabilities.
Music v2 also gives you granular control for how you work with the model.
For brands and content teams, ElevenCreative Music handles licensed music at scale.
Music v2 is the engine underneath.
Built for musicians, developers, and brands Music v2 is the model powering products across three ElevenLabs platforms, each built for a different use case.
For developers, ElevenAPI gives you direct access to the model.
And across languages, lyrics, vocals, and arrangements now perform more reliably in the language you write in, with meaningful improvements across a growing list of supported languages.
